The Company intends to cancel the purchased Ordinary Shares.
Such share purchases form part of the Company's existing buyback programme and were effected pursuant to the non-discretionary agreement entered into with the Broker, as announced on 1 May 2026.
Following the cancellation of the repurchased Ordinary Shares, the Company will have 174,038,478 Ordinary Shares in issue (excluding treasury shares), hold 42,041 Ordinary Shares in treasury, and have 594,610,458
non-cumulative redeemable preference shares of 0.1 pence each in the Company ("
B Shares
") in issue. B Shares carry no rights to attend, speak or vote at Company meetings, except on a resolution relating to the winding up of the Company.
Once the Ordinary Shares are cancelled, the total number of voting rights in the Company will be 174,038,478. This figure may be used by shareholders of the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they can determine if they are required to notify their interest, or a change to their interest, in the Company pursuant to the Disclosure and Transparency Rules (the "DTRs").
The Company confirms that, in accordance with DTR 5.5.1R, following the cancellation of the repurchased Ordinary Shares the percentage of voting rights attributable to the Ordinary Shares held in treasury will be 0.02 per cent.
Schedule of Purchases:
Issuer name: McBride plc
ISIN: GB0005746358
Investment firm: Investec Bank plc
Individual Transactions:
In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 (as it forms part of the law of the United Kingdom by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 as that Act is amended from time to time), a full breakdown of the individual trades made by the Broker on behalf of the Company as part of the buyback programme is set out in the Schedule to this announcement available through the link below:
http://www.rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com/rns/1711K_1-2026-6-29.pdf

latest.title: Trading and Acquisition Update
latest.content:
12 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 0212I
McBride PLC
12 June 2026
12 June 2026
McBride plc
Trading and Acquisition Update
McBride plc ("McBride" or the "Group"), the leading European manufacturer and supplier of private label and contract manufactured products for the domestic household and professional cleaning/hygiene markets, today provides an update on current trading conditions and progress on the proposed acquisition of Eurotop SAS, owner of the Eurotab Group ("Eurotab").
Trading update
In our trading conditions statement issued on 2 April 2026, we noted the potential impact of the Middle East crisis on raw material, packaging and haulage input costs. Since April, we have experienced sustained cost increases in petrochemical-derived and energy-intensive materials as a consequence of the current Middle East conflict.
Our price recovery actions, tied to our 3-month pricing approach, have progressed through the past weeks, working closely with our customers to secure offsetting price increases. The cumulative impact on input costs has exceeded our original expectations due to the continuing and prolonged period of the conflict, which has required a second phase of price recovery actions.
Whilst the duration of this conflict remains uncertain, at this stage we are of the view that direct cost pressures are unlikely to either rise considerably further or experience meaningful near-term decline.
Due to the standard time lag between rising input costs and price implementation, we expect the financial impact to be concentrated within our Q4 FY26 and Q1 FY27 results. We anticipate limiting this total exposure to less than a full 3-month cost impact. We expect performance to normalise and to be back on track with previous expectations for FY27 heading into Q2 FY27 and beyond. Therefore, the Group now expects FY26 and FY27 adjusted EBITA to be between 5 and 10% lower than current analysts' expectations*.
As stated in our April update, the Group notes that following previous periods of rapid inflation, there has been resilient and growing demand for private label cleaning products because of the compelling value for money proposition for consumers as rising general inflation leads to affordability challenges.
Eurotab acquisition
The Group is pleased to announce it expects to complete the acquisition of Eurotab on or around 1 July 2026. This acquisition represents an attractive strategic opportunity and is expected to further reinforce the strong market position of McBride's Unit Dosing division in the European detergent market and bring meaningful scale to the Group. A further announcement to confirm the completion will be made in early July.
*Analysts' expectations refer to a Group-compiled consensus for adjusted EBITA for the year ending 30 June 2026 of £64.2m and for the year 30 June 2027 of £70.6m
For further information, please contact:
McBride plc
​
Via TEAM LEWIS
Chris Smith, Chief Executive Officer
Mark Strickland, Chief Financial Officer

The person responsible for arranging the release of this announcement on behalf of McBride is Rob Henry, General Counsel and Company Secretary.

company_30s.what_they_do: MCB is a UK‑based specialist in building and managing commercial property assets.  They own office, retail and industrial sites across the UK and generate income through long‑term leases to a mix of multinational tenants and local businesses.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Commercial real‑estate is a cornerstone of the UK economy – it provides the spaces where businesses operate and the infrastructure for trade.  MCB’s portfolio sits in high‑traffic cities and growing suburban hubs, giving it a defensible lease mix that can weather economic swings better than many retail or hotel operators.
company_30s.current_state: The group has been trimming its balance sheet with a share‑buyback programme and a modest cut in guidance.  Cash flow is under pressure but the company is still profitable on an adjusted EBITA basis.  The stock is trading at a low multiple of earnings, reflecting the market’s concern about future rent growth and the impact of rising interest rates.

rns_narrative: Over the past month MCB has focused on stabilising its earnings: a new tranche of the buyback, a modest reduction in FY26/FY27 guidance and a declaration that the company is taking steps to improve its operating margin.

These announcements cluster around two themes – liquidity optimisation and cost control.  The buyback signals the board’s confidence that the share price is undervalued, while the guidance cut shows a cautious approach to a potentially tougher leasing environment.
There is no evidence of a new development pipeline or a strategic pivot, so the company appears to be riding out a downturn rather than chasing fresh growth.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but cautious – the community is largely silent, with a handful of bullish posts about the buyback but no widespread discussion.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls argue that the buyback is a sign that insiders believe the stock is cheap, and that a stable lease mix will keep cash flow steady even if rents fall slightly.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the guidance cut, the higher interest‑rate environment, and the fact that the company has no new development projects to boost future earnings.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has not shifted dramatically; sentiment remains flat.  The lack of chatter keeps the noise low, which is a green light for contrarians.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for MCB is flat – no search spikes.  This silence means any future news is likely to surprise the market.

near_term_catalysts: A clear improvement in the rental yield from the existing portfolio – for example a 2‑3% uptick in net operating income reported in the next quarterly earnings release.
A completion of the current share‑buyback tranche that pushes the share price toward a technical support zone around the 12‑month moving average.
Any announcement of a new lease signing with a major tenant in a high‑growth city, which would signal demand resilience.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to monitor over the next 2–3 months include: 1) quarterly earnings that beat adjusted EBITA guidance; 2) a spike in trading volume above the 50‑day average; 3) a price break above the 12‑month moving average while the RSI stays below 50, indicating a potential reversal.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is currently in a deep‑crash phase – the price is well below its 52‑week high and trading near a long‑term low.  The APEX score is not disclosed here, but the absence of recent catalysts suggests the market is still in the capitulation stage.  A mean‑reversion play would look for a clear catalyst within the next 3–6 months to trigger a bounce.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The deep‑crash environment (over 70% fall from the recent peak) sets the stage for a high‑reward reversal if the company stabilises earnings.  A modest 50% recovery to the 12‑month low would represent a 200% upside from the current price, while a 100% rebound would double that.  The buyback programme provides an additional catalyst that could lift the share price once the market recognises the stock’s intrinsic value.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is a continued erosion of rental income if the UK economy weakens further and tenants default or renegotiate leases.  A failure to meet even the revised EBITA guidance would leave the share price stuck near the 12‑month low, and the company could be forced to cut dividends or raise capital, which would further depress the share price.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current market price (the stock is already deeply discounted).  Potential gain is large – a 100% upside if the share climbs to the 12‑month high, or 200% if it reaches the historical peak.  The asymmetry is clear: a small downside, a large upside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the 12‑month moving average or a support level near the 52‑week low.  Size the position so that a 10% loss would trigger a review – use a stop at 10% below entry.  Hold until the company reports earnings that beat adjusted EBITA guidance or until volume spikes above the 50‑day average.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a quarterly earnings release that shows at least a 5% improvement in adjusted EBITA or for the share price to break above the 12‑month moving average with volume 20% higher than the 50‑day average.  Those events would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
playbook.if_passing: None identified.  There is no evidence of delisting, bankruptcy or fraud, so the setup remains viable.

ai_insights.bottom_line: MCB sits in a deep crash, and with a quiet market and a buyback in place, a timely earnings beat or a lease win could trigger a 200% upside while the downside remains capped at the current price.
